brief.name: RE-1 — boutique off-market acquisitions team (LA)  // JOHNNY-EDITABLE name/handle
brief.what_they_do: Founder-led team (2-5) doing off-market / distressed acquisitions in predetermined key areas of LA. Just completed a major project cycle and closed 14 properties in the last 12 months. Researches owners manually (title runs, BeenVerified-style lookups, driving-for-dollars). Holds a raw ~10k-row county data CSV. No CRM or automation tooling. Wants to expand to other cities.

signals.detail: we finally sold everything on it. So right now I have like no houses
The way that we've typically found distressed properties is straight up just driving
Our email thing has been like consistently 30% wrong
fifteen thousand dollars and it's like 40 enriched... this is just way too much so... we'll stick doing it manually
either we need to hire someone else on the team or i could get... like an assistant... the goal is is to start moving to other cities

i'm johnny — an independent AI-automations marketer. i build the data+story layer for sales teams: scraping, enrichment, CRM-from-scratch, agent systems. receipts: built Vortex for a marketing agency on a signed $5,040 two-month contract; my LinkedIn outreach pulled ~40% reply rates vs 2% cold email; for a real-estate team i designed the off-market pipeline — owner age, sale dates, life-stage reads — the same method that closed 14 deals in LA off a 10k-row county CSV.
PERSON SCRAPE DEGRADED: person stage skipped: no person inputs on the run (linkedinUrl/xHandle/name all absent)
Real-estate investor client agreed $4-5k flat + 10-20% commission ('anywhere from 4 5 K would be sick')
Marketing agency signed fixed $5,040 / 2 months / 4 modules + ~$250/mo running costs
'name + linkedin' Google-search hack: ~85% hit rate where scrapers get accounts flagged
LinkedIn outreach ~40% reply rate vs cold email ~2% ('if you're doing like good')
The pricing anchor to beat: refused vendor at '$15,000 and it's like 40 enriched'

brief.brief: Carlos-shaped primary: pipeline gap + manual stack + 30%-wrong data + refused $15k vendor + city-expansion ambition. The system replaces the hire.
brief.provenance: fixture · authored verbatim from the Carlos discovery-call corpus (guided, not faked)

event.story:
THE GOAL:
Close the pipeline gap fast and take the off-market playbook that just produced 14 deals in LA into new cities — without hiring a full-time researcher or rebuilding the manual stack city by city. In Carlos's own words: "the goal is to start moving to other cities." [expansion_intent]

THE OBSTACLE:
The 10k-row county CSV is the engine, but it's running on bad fuel. Email data is "consistently 30% wrong" [data_pain], which means every outreach sequence bleeds leads before it starts. The team is driving for dollars and running title lookups by hand [manual_research] — a process that worked at 14 deals/year in one market but doesn't scale to a second city without adding headcount. Right now there are literally no houses in the pipeline: "we finally sold everything on it. So right now I have like no houses." [pipeline_gap] The gap isn't ambition — it's the data layer underneath it.

THE OLD WAY FAILS:
Carlos already stress-tested the alternative and walked away. A vendor quoted $15,000 for 40 enriched records — and the answer was "this is just way too much so... we'll stick doing it manually." [vendor_refusal] Doing it manually meant driving neighborhoods, running BeenVerified-style lookups one by one, and absorbing a 30% email error rate on every campaign. [manual_research, data_pain] That approach produced 14 deals, but it also produced a pipeline that just hit zero the moment the project cycle ended. Hiring an assistant or a new team member [expansion_intent] patches the labor problem but doesn't fix the data quality problem — and it resets to zero again in city two.

THE BETTER PATH:
Johnny built the exact system Carlos is describing — not a vendor pitch, a working receipt. The off-market pipeline he designed for a real-estate team used owner age, sale dates, and life-stage reads on a 10k-row county CSV; that same method produced the 14 LA deals Carlos just closed. [seller_positioning, seller_proof] His enrichment approach — the 'name + LinkedIn' Google-search hack — hits ~85% where scrapers get accounts flagged and banned. [seller_pack] The output isn't a one-time data drop; it's a durable, rerunnable CRM-style system that doesn't need to be rebuilt every two months when the market shifts. Pricing is structured the way Carlos already said feels right: flat base ($4–5k range) plus commission on deals sourced from the system — not $15,000 for 40 rows. [seller_proof, vendor_refusal]

Opening line (Johnny's voice): "You just closed out a full cycle with nothing left in the pipe — that's actually the best moment to build the system, because you can design it clean for LA *and* city two at the same time."

THE BETTER OUTCOME:
Carlos's team runs the same 10k-row county CSV through a clean enrichment layer — owner age flags, life-stage reads, verified contact data — and the 30% email error rate drops to noise. [data_pain] The pipeline refills without a new hire, because the system does the research job. When the expansion to city two happens, the playbook is already built: pull the new county CSV, run it through the same stack, and the team hits the ground with qualified, story-tagged leads instead of starting from scratch with a car and a notepad. [expansion_intent, manual_research] Carlos stays the decision-maker on which doors to knock — the system just makes sure every door on the list is worth knocking.
